chapter_goal: Read moment-map convexity as a visible fixed-point and connected-fiber theorem for Hamiltonian torus actions.

source_span_read.source_map_note: The inventory span 182-188 starts at printed page 172 and spills into Lecture 28; page headers show 178-184 is the usable Convexity span.

concept_inventory: Atiyah-Guillemin-Sternberg convexity theorem for compact Hamiltonian torus actions
induction statements A_m for connected fibers and B_m for convex images
integer injective matrix A selecting an (m-1)-subtorus and projected moment map A^T mu
fixed-point components C_j with constant moment values eta_j
separating functional with rationally independent direction X
effective actions, lower bound m+1 fixed points, and dim M >= 2m
examples: S2 height interval, CP1 interval, CP2 triangle, CP3 simplex, CP1 x CP1 rectangle

visual_sequence.representation: NetworkX directed dependency graph rendered with Matplotlib
visual_sequence.inspection_target: follow A_m, B_m, subtorus slicing, and fixed-point hull dependencies
visual_sequence.validation: directed acyclic graph; theorem nodes are reachable from source hypotheses

visual_sequence.representation: Matplotlib interval, triangle, and tetrahedron projection
visual_sequence.inspection_target: fixed-point images are the vertices and sampled moment values fill their convex hulls
visual_sequence.validation: Delaunay/hull containment residuals for CP2 and CP3 samples

visual_sequence.representation: Matplotlib affine line slice inside the CP2 triangle
visual_sequence.inspection_target: a connected projected level maps into a line and contains every segment value
visual_sequence.validation: all slice samples have constant A^T mu within tolerance

visual_sequence.inspection_target: interior, edge, and vertex strata show stabilizer growth toward fixed points
visual_sequence.validation: CP2 has m+1 fixed points and dim CP2 = 2m

visual_sequence.representation: Plotly HTML with CP2 and CP3 moment images
visual_sequence.inspection_target: rotate the CP3 tetrahedron and compare it with the CP2 triangular face
visual_sequence.validation: HTML file exists and the same hull checks pass in JSON

proof_visualization_strategy: Use a dependency graph for the global theorem, a line-slice diagram for the induction step A_{m-1} => B_m, and a separating-functional ledger for the fixed-point convex-hull converse.
computational_checks: convex hull containment for sampled CP2 and CP3 moment-map values
constant projected moment value along the selected subtorus slice
fixed-point counts and dimension inequality for the effective CP2 action
artifact existence and nonzero file size
